asset_name: Spell Lane and Reflection Line — approach path, judgment line, and lane glow
shared_properties: The lane uses the arena center band and stays visible over the stage shell without hiding characters.
Reflection cues rely on cyan, lime, and gold accents from the duel UI palette.
The judgment line must read during both slow ice orbs and fast light bolts.
differences: This task covers world-space lane overlays rather than HUD-space timing widgets.
Projectile bodies, telegraph icons, and card UI remain in separate tasks.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Moodboard for placing luminous lane overlays inside the warm academy room without losing contrast.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_player_silhouette
reference_images.purpose: Player silhouette board for validating the judgment line position relative to the student combat root.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load assets/art/reflect_academy/trial_2/world_spell_lane_reflection_line.png during DuelLoading and cache the atlas layout under world_spell_lane_reflection_line.
runtime_notes: Use four atlas states named idle, telegraph, incoming, and reflected to match lane feedback timing.
Anchor the lane overlay at x=960 y=540 so the judgment line lands 20px in front of the player collision root.
Blend the lane glow with additive material or emissive tint while preserving the room art below it.

context.usage: World-space lane art that shows where enemy spells travel and where the reflection timing line sits.

shape.silhouette: Long diagonal spell lane with a gold-edged guide strip, cyan reflection line, and soft magic haze.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 5760x220px with four 1440x220 states and no padding.
The judgment line aligns to the documented player-side hit check 20px before impact.
Telegraph, incoming, and reflected states remain visually distinct with readable cyan and lime separation.
Lane glow never obscures character faces or HP bars when composited in the final scene.
